title: Wise Parenthood
description: Wise Parenthood is a non-fiction by Marie Stopes.
descriptionParagraphs: Wise Parenthood is a non-fiction by Marie Stopes. The current record survives chiefly through censorship documentation rather than a full English-language critical apparatus.

The surviving record is interesting because it shows how even ordinary-looking books can acquire a charged political afterlife. As a non-fiction, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in New Zealand. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says:  More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

overview.englishDescription: Wise Parenthood is a non-fiction by Marie Stopes.
banningParagraphs: Wise Parenthood entered censorship debates as a non-fiction associated with controversy, publication history, and state scrutiny. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around political sensitivity.

The earliest event currently captured here is 20th century in New Zealand, where Customs Department classified, prohibited, or restricted. Authorities treated the work as objectionable under the current New Zealand censorship record. This imported record still needs fuller local archival detail.
This entry is still incomplete: more jurisdictions, court orders, and translated justifications should be added over time.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.
counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
counterReadings.note: Useful for comparing older obscenity, heresy, and political bans with modern free-speech disputes.
